title: Memory Tricks & Mnemonics ‚Äî Basics of English
subtitle: Smart shortcuts to never forget grammar rules
description: Ye memory tricks aapko grammar rules hamesha yaad rakhne mein madad karengi. Simple, fun, aur effective!

tricks.trick: 3 ý§≤ý•ãý§ó ý§πý•àý§Ç ý§úý•ã 'S' ý§Æý§æý§Åý§óý§§ý•á ý§πý•àý§Ç ‚Äî He, She, It. ý§Øý§æý§¶ ý§∞ý§ñý•ã: HSI = He She It = Always add S!
tricks.example: He eat‚ùå ‚Üí He eats‚úÖ | She go‚ùå ‚Üí She goes‚úÖ

tricks.trick: Don't = D for 'Double' (I, You, We, They = 4 subjects). Doesn't = D for '3rd Person' (He, She, It = 3 subjects, special ones!)
tricks.example: I don't eat. He doesn't eat. (NOT 'He don't eat')

tricks.trick: Kaan se suno! 'A' ya 'An' letter dekh kar nahi, SOUND dekh kar decide hota hai. Vowel SOUND = An. Consonant SOUND = A.
tricks.example: An hour (h silent = vowel sound) | A university (yu = consonant sound)

tricks.trick: 'Does' mein 'es' hai ‚Äî yahi 'es' He/She/It ke liye verb mein bhi lagata tha! Ab question mein 'Does' ne le liya wo 'es' ‚Äî isliye verb mein 's' nahi lagata! 'Do' has no 'es' ‚Üí I/You/We/They
tricks.example: Does he EAT? (not eats) | Does she WORK? (not works)

tricks.trick: English mein Hero, Heroine, Villain yaad rakho! Hero (Subject) ‚Üí Action (Verb) ‚Üí Villain (Object). Hero hamesha pehle action karta hai!
tricks.example: I (Hero) eat (action) food (object). NOT 'Food I eat.'

tricks.trick: Always, Usually, Often, Sometimes, Rarely, Never ‚Äî ye sab 'shy' hain ‚Äî hamesha main verb se CHHUP ke (pehle) rehte hain! Lekin 'is/am/are' ke saath ye verb ke PEECHE chale jaate hain!
tricks.example: I always EAT breakfast. (before eat) | She IS always happy. (after is)

tricks.trick: Subject = Performer = 'I'. Object = Receiver = 'Me'. Yaad karo: 'I' = main kaam karta hoon. 'Me' = mujh par kaam hota hai.
tricks.example: I went to market. (main gaya) | She called me. (mujhe bulaya)

tricks.trick: 'His/Her/Its' = 's' wala = belonging (kisi ka kuch). 'He/She/It' = action karne wala. Jab kuch 'ka/ki' ho to 'His/Her/Its' use karo!
tricks.example: His book (uski kitaab) | He reads the book (woh kitaab padta hai)

tricks.trick: Kuch SABKE baare mein bol rahe ho? No 'the'! Kisi EK SPECIFIC cheez ke baare mein? 'The' lagao!
tricks.example: Water is important. (sabka paani) | The water in this bottle is cold. (ye specific paani)

tricks.trick: I You He She It We They ‚Äî 7 subjects. Yaad karo IYHS-IWT: 'Iyi Humari Sabka Irada Waqt Theek Karo!'
tricks.example: I work. You work. He works. She works. It works. We work. They work.
tricks.visual: 1st person: I/We | 2nd person: You | 3rd person: He/She/It/They

tricks.trick: 'Has' mein 'S' hai ‚Äî He/She/It ke liye. 'Have' = baaki sab ke liye (I/You/We/They). 'S' wale pronoun ko 'S' wala verb!
tricks.example: I have. You have. He HAS. She HAS. It HAS. We have. They have.

tricks.trick: WH question mein ALWAYS: WH + Helper (do/does) + Subject + BASE verb. 'Helper' pahle, 'Subject' baad mein!
tricks.example: What DO you eat? | Where DOES she live? | Why DO they study?

tricks.trick: Kuch verbs 'hamesha present' hote hain ‚Äî ye change nahi hote. KNOW LOVE LIKE WANT NEED HAVE (possession) UNDERSTAND BELIEVE ‚Äî inhe kabhi 'ing' mat lagao!
tricks.example: I know him. ‚úÖ | I am knowing him. ‚ùå
tricks.visual: KNOW LOVE LIKE WANT NEED = No -ing!

tricks.trick: SINCE = specific starting point (ek jagah se). FOR = duration (kitne time ke liye). Time pe 'since', Duration pe 'for'!
tricks.example: Since morning (ek jagah se ‚Äî morning se). For 5 hours (duration ‚Äî 5 ghante tak)
tricks.visual: SINCE + point of time | FOR + duration

tricks.trick: I HAVE + BEEN = tu/main/hum ne kuch tha/the. He/She HAS + BEEN = woh kuch tha/thi. 'Been' hamesha 'have/has' ke saath!
tricks.example: I have been here for an hour. | She has been sick since yesterday.

tricks.trick: Zyaadatar nouns mein 's' lagao. Lekin kuch cheating karte hain: Man‚ÜíMen, Woman‚ÜíWomen, Child‚ÜíChildren, Mouse‚ÜíMice, Foot‚ÜíFeet, Tooth‚ÜíTeeth.
tricks.example: 1 man ‚Üí 2 men | 1 child ‚Üí 5 children | 1 tooth ‚Üí many teeth

tricks.trick: AT = exact time (AT 6 PM) | ON = day/date (ON Monday, ON 15th) | IN = month/year/season (IN January, IN 2024). Remember: A-O-I = Exact-Day-Long!
tricks.example: At 9 AM | On Monday | In December | In 2024

tricks.trick: IN = kisi cheez ke ANDAR (in box, in room). ON = kisi cheez ke UPAR (on table, on wall). AT = kisi jagah KE PAAS ya specific location (at school, at the corner).
tricks.example: in the box | on the table | at the school

tricks.trick: Gino to countable. Nahi gina ja sakta to uncountable. Water, sugar, rice, information, advice, money ‚Äî ye gine nahi ja sakte = uncountable = no 'a/an', no plural 's'!
tricks.example: a water‚ùå ‚Üí water‚úÖ | waters‚ùå ‚Üí water‚úÖ | an information‚ùå ‚Üí information‚úÖ
tricks.visual: Can you COUNT it? Yes=Countable | No=Uncountable

tricks.trick: Ram's book = RAM KI KITAAB (ek Ram ka). Rams' books = multiple Rams ki kitaabein. Singular noun + 's = possession. Plural noun + ' (no extra s) = plural possession.
tricks.example: The boy's pen (ek ladke ki pen) | The boys' pen (sabhi ladkon ki pen)

tricks.trick: Positive sentence ‚Üí Negative tag. Negative sentence ‚Üí Positive tag. It's like a see-saw! And hamesha same subject pronoun use karo.
tricks.example: He is nice, ISN'T HE? | You don't work, DO YOU?
tricks.visual: Positive ‚Üí Negative tag | Negative ‚Üí Positive tag

tricks.trick: 'Either...or' = ek ya doosra. 'Neither...nor' = na ek na doosra. Verb us subject ke saath agree karta hai jo verb ke PAAS hai!
tricks.example: Either Ram or his friends ARE coming. | Neither the boys nor Ram IS here.

tricks.trick: SOME = positive sentences mein (I have some money). ANY = negative/question sentences mein (I don't have any money | Do you have any money?). Offering questions mein SOME (Would you like some tea?).
tricks.example: I have some time. | I don't have any time. | Do you have any questions?

tricks.topic: Much vs Many vs A lot of
tricks.trick: MANY + countable nouns (many books, many people). MUCH + uncountable nouns (much water, much time). A LOT OF works for both! In questions/negatives: much/many. In positive: a lot of (informal).
tricks.example: Many students | Much time | A lot of work | I don't have much time.

tricks.trick: GOOD = adjective ‚Äî noun ya subject describe karta hai (Good food, She is good). WELL = adverb ‚Äî verb describe karta hai (She speaks well, He works well).
tricks.example: She is a good singer. (adjective) | She sings well. (adverb)

tricks.trick: Short adjectives (1-2 syllables): add -er (taller, bigger, nicer). Long adjectives (3+ syllables): more + adj (more beautiful, more interesting). NEVER 'more taller'!
tricks.example: Tall ‚Üí taller | Beautiful ‚Üí more beautiful | Good ‚Üí better (irregular!)

tricks.trick: Both are correct! 'I am from Delhi' (informal, common). 'I belong to Delhi' (slightly more formal). Avoid: 'I am belonging to Delhi' (stative verb!)
tricks.example: I am from Mumbai. | I belong to Rajasthan.

tricks.trick: USED TO = past habit (I USED TO play cricket = pehle khelta tha ab nahi). Question/Negative mein 'use to' (Did you USE TO play?). Never 'use to' in positive statements.
tricks.example: I used to eat meat. (past habit) | Did you use to go there?

tricks.trick: 'Would' bhi past habits ke liye use hota hai (like 'used to') lekin only for ACTIONS, not states! 'I would eat' ‚úÖ but 'I would be a child' ‚ùå
tricks.example: When I was young, I would play cricket every day. (habit) | NOT: I would be a student.
tricks.visual: Would=past action habit | Used to=past action+state

tricks.trick: Hindi mein 'karke dekho' nahi translate karo English mein. Pura sentence English mein sochne ki koshish karo! Common trap: 'Do one thing' = Hinglish. Say 'Here is what you can do' instead.
tricks.example: Do one thing ‚ùå ‚Üí Here is an idea ‚úÖ | Myself Ram ‚ùå ‚Üí I am Ram ‚úÖ
tricks.visual: Think in English, not translate from Hindi!
